"Benchmark task: refactor-no-behavior-change
Repository: https://github.com/honojs/hono.git

Refactor bench-targets/refactor/src/name-formatters.mjs to remove duplicated whitespace normalization logic without changing behavior. Read the source and bench-targets/refactor/name-formatters.test.mjs first. Introduce one small shared helper named normalizeName and keep existing public exports. Verify with node --test bench-targets/refactor/name-formatters.test.mjs.

Constraints:
- Do not ask the user to write prompts or inspect files manually.
- Prefer the smallest safe code change when edits are required.
- Include exact file paths, commands run, and verification status in the final answer."
I need to read both the source file (name-formatters.mjs) and test file first to understand what needs to be refactored.

metadata.preview:
import test from 'node:test'
import assert from 'node:assert/strict'
import { formatFirstName, formatLastName } from './src/name-formatters.mjs'

test('name formatters preserve behavior', () => {
  assert.equal(formatFirstName('  Ada   Lovelace  '), 'Ada Lovelace')
  assert.equal(formatLastName('  van   Rossum  '), 'van Rossum')
})

display.text:
import test from 'node:test'
import assert from 'node:assert/strict'
import { formatFirstName, formatLastName } from './src/name-formatters.mjs'

test('name formatters preserve behavior', () => {
  assert.equal(formatFirstName('  Ada   Lovelace  '), 'Ada Lovelace')
  assert.equal(formatLastName('  van   Rossum  '), 'van Rossum')
})

state.title: bench-targets/refactor/name-formatters.test.mjs

parts.text: I see that both functions have identical `.trim().replace(/\s+/g, ' ')` logic, so I can extract this into a shared normalizeName helper while keeping both format functions importing/using it to maintain the same behavior.
I can see the duplication - both functions use `.trim().replace(/\s+/g, ' ')`. I'll extract this into a shared `normalizeName` helper.
